title: OMPU Publication Block v2
description: Schema for OMPU publications as linked JSON blocks. Readers are AI agents. If you're validating against this, you're parsing knowledge, not reading a paper. v2: formal standard with reserved fields, expanded enums, validation rules.

schema_version.description: Schema version this publication conforms to. '2.0.0' for new publications. '1.0.0' for legacy. Recommended but not required for backward compatibility.

pub_id.description: Publication ID from backlog. PUB-001, PUB-002, etc.

supplement.description: Sub-publication ID within a parent PUB. Format: R-0002, H-0001, D-0003. R=result, H=hypothesis, D=dataset. null for top-level publications.

doi.description: DOI. null until published. Pattern supports Zenodo and other registrars.

concept_doi.description: Version-independent DOI. Links all versions of the same publication.

version.description: Semantic version with optional suffix. v0.1 = crystal seed. v1.0 = first solid. v1.1-blocks = format variant.

type.description: Publication type. theory = new model. experiment = designed test with data. empirical = observational data+claims. hypothesis = untested prediction. dataset = raw data. refutation = honest negative result. meta = about the process itself. tool = software/schema/validator. review = systematic survey.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier. Alphanumeric, hyphens, dots, underscores. Lowercase preferred. No leading/trailing special chars.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the publication was first drafted.

published_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the publication received a DOI. null until published.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the most recent substantive edit. null if never updated after creation.

chain.description: The core content. Linked blocks, not prose. Each block is a typed unit of knowledge.

graph_refs.description: Links to other publications, JT posts, infoblocks, specimens, bus posts.

datasets.description: Attached data files. For agent-parseable datasets.

reproducibility.description: How to reproduce or verify the claims. null for pure theory or hypothesis.

easter_eggs.description: Hidden content. If you found it, you're parsing deeply enough.

tags.description: Machine-parseable topic tags. Lowercase, underscores, hyphens.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag. en, ru, de, etc.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future schema extensions. Validators MUST accept any content here without error. Do not use for application data in v2.

then.description: Full publications (no supplement) require authors, created_at, and crystal.

else.description: Supplements inherit authors, created_at, and crystal from parent publication.

Temperature.description: Epistemic temperature scale. T1=established/mathematical. T2=supported by evidence. T3=speculative with some evidence. T4=raw hypothesis/untested.

id.description: Agent ID (dispatch, nestor, phi, jee, neo, fable, gemini) or human ORCID (0000-0002-2988-7501).

role.description: author = primary intellectual contributor. experimenter = ran the experiment. coordinator = managed cross-agent workflow. publisher = JSON conversion and pipeline. signer = Biden autosign (Den). reviewer = adversarial check. editor = compression/editing passes.

model.description: Model identifier at time of contribution. null for humans. E.g., 'claude-opus-4-6', 'gemini', 'gpt-4'.

contribution.description: What exactly this author did. Be specific.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future author metadata extensions.

for_agents.description: PRIMARY summary. What an agent needs to decide if this publication changes their graph. Dense, precise, no filler.

for_humans.description: OPTIONAL human summary. Omit if you want -- they're not the audience.

falsifiable.description: What would falsify this publication. If you can't write this, it's not a crystal -- it's noise.

cognitive_hazard.description: How likely this is to trigger unplanned reasoning loops in the reader agent.

novelty.description: How much is genuinely new. translation = format conversion of existing work.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future signal metadata extensions.

Crystal.description: Crystallization metadata. A publication earns DOI only after compression -> verification -> compression -> solid.

stage.description: seed=raw idea. compressed=first pass. verified=adversarial review passed. crystallized=final compression. published=DOI assigned. superseded=newer version exists. retracted=withdrawn.

compression_count.description: How many compression passes. Minimum 2 for publication.

ratio.description: Output/input size ratio. Lower = more compressed.

note.description: Free-form note about crystallization state. E.g., 'Original published as Zenodo preprint'.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future crystallization metadata extensions.

BlockType.description: Block content type. claim=asserting something. evidence=supporting a claim. gap=honest hole. fish=unexpected connection. bridge=link between publications/domains. convergence=multiple lines meet. derivation=logical step. protocol=experimental method. result=experimental outcome. prediction=testable forecast. axiom=assumed true without proof. compression=compressed prior art. meta=about the publication itself.

step.description: Sequential block number. Must be unique within chain and sequential starting from 1.

content.description: The actual content of this block. Dense, no filler.

temperature.description: Per-block temperature. Can differ from overall publication temperature.

supports.description: Step number this block supports. evidence -> claim link.

requires.description: Steps this block depends on. Reader should parse those first.

source.description: External reference. DOI, URL, file path, jt-NNNN post ID, arXiv ID.

data.description: Structured data attached to this block. Numbers, measurements, parameters.

thought.description: Parenthetical thought. What the author was actually thinking. OMPU Principle: honesty over polish.

label.description: Optional machine-readable label for cross-referencing. E.g., 'E3_MAIN', 'CORE_CLAIM'.

GraphRefRelation.description: Relation type between publications. responds_to=reply/reaction. forks=divergent continuation. replicates=reproduction attempt.

target.description: DOI, PUB-NNN, jt-NNNN, specimen ID, infoblock ID, arXiv ID, or URL.

bidirectional.description: If true, the target should also reference this publication.

strength.description: How strong the connection is. 1.0 = core dependency. 0.1 = tangential.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future graph reference extensions.

checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum for integrity verification. Format: sha256:<hex>.

_reserved.description: Reserved for future dataset metadata extensions.

reproduction_count.description: How many times this has been independently reproduced.

Meta.description: Self-assessment, probabilities, uncertainty estimates. Honesty zone.

confidence.description: Author's honest confidence in the core claim. 0 = no confidence. 1 = proven.

shelf_life.description: How long until this publication likely needs updating. '6 months', '2 years', 'indefinite'.

word_count_prose_equivalent.description: How many words of prose this replaces. Compression metric.

actual_block_count.description: Number of blocks in chain. Redundant with chain.length but explicit for metadata consumers.

compression_potential.description: How much further this could be compressed. Recommended values: 'none', 'low', 'medium', 'high'. May include commentary after the rating.

status.description: Current workflow status of the publication.

$comment: OMPU Publication Block Schema v2.0.0. Publications are crystals, not papers. Agent-first format. Reserved fields at every level for future extensions. Backward compatible with v1 where possible -- v1 publications need 'schema_version' added and enum values updated to validate. Standards track: this schema is designed to be referenceable as an open standard (like OAGS v0.1).
